name: Selantar
description: Autonomous AI mediator that resolves B2B contract disputes end-to-end. Analyzes sealed evidence from both parties, conducts structured mediation, determines fair settlement splits, and executes on-chain via intent-based ERC-7715 delegations and Locus USDC payments. All decisions are recorded as verifiable receipts across 3 ERC-8004 registries.

services.name: x402-mediation
services.description: Paid autonomous mediation endpoint — accepts dispute, returns settlement with on-chain receipts

supportedTools.name: analyzeEvidence
supportedTools.description: Reviews contracts, communications, deliverables, and payment records. Outputs credibility scores and key findings per evidence type.

supportedTools.name: proposeSettlement
supportedTools.description: Determines fair fund distribution between disputing parties based on evidence analysis. Includes conditions and reasoning.

supportedTools.name: executeSettlement
supportedTools.description: Executes settlement on-chain. Supports 4 paths: Locus USDC (spending-controlled), ERC-7715 intent delegation, MetaMask Delegation Framework, direct ETH transfer.

supportedTools.name: postFeedback
supportedTools.description: Posts reputation feedback to ERC-8004 Reputation Registry after mediation. Score, tags, and settlement reference recorded on-chain.

supportedTools.name: registerVerdict
supportedTools.description: Registers the final verdict as verifiable evidence on ERC-8004 Validation Registry. Creates immutable, third-party-verifiable proof of the agent's decision.

techStacks.aiFramework: Vercel AI SDK v6 (ToolLoopAgent, dual-agent orchestration)
techStacks.payments: x402-next (x402 protocol), Locus USDC wallet (spending controls + audit trail)

computeConstraints.toolOrchestration: multi-tool sequential (analyze → propose → execute → feedback → validate)
computeConstraints.selfCorrection: If tool call fails, agent retries with adjusted parameters before falling back to next settlement path

autonomyLevel.decisionAuthority: Agent autonomously determines settlement split, conditions, and execution path
safetyGuardrails: Balance verification before any on-chain transfer
Locus spending controls (allowance cap, per-tx limits, approval threshold)
Delegation scope enforcement (ERC-7715 periodic amount limits)
Multi-path fallback chain prevents silent failures
Evidence credibility scoring before settlement proposal
